info.name: Clappia Public APIs
info.description:
Official Postman collection for Clappia's Public REST APIs. Build powerful integrations with Clappia's no-code platform.

## Setup

1. Set your `apiKey` in collection variables (get it from [Clappia Workplace settings](https://www.clappia.com/help/clappia-public-apis))
    
2. Set your `workplaceId` in collection variables
    
3. Update `baseUrl` if needed (defaults to the production URL)
    

## Authentication

All requests use API Key authentication via `x-api-key` header.

## Support

Feel free to reach out to use if you need any help with setting up API Integrations. Follow [this guide](https://www.clappia.com/help/request-support) to know about the different support channels.

Visit [www.clappia.com](https://www.clappia.com) to know more.

item.description:
Fetch complete details of a specific submission including:

- All field values and metadata
    
- Submission owners and timestamps
    
- Current submission status
    

**Required Fields:**

- appId: Unique identifier of your Clappia App
    
- workplaceId: Your Clappia Workplace ID
    
- submissionId: Unique Submission ID of the Submission which is to be retrieved

item.description:
**Bulk Data Retrieval & Filtering**

Advanced submission querying with:

- **Filtering:** By status, dates, custom fields
    
- **Pagination:** Handle large datasets efficiently
    
- **Sorting:** Order by any field or timestamp
    
- **Complex Logic:** AND/OR conditions, nested queries
    

**Operators:** EQ, GTE, LTE, CONTAINS, IN, NOT_IN

**Required Fields:**

- appId: Unique identifier of your Clappia App
    
- workplaceId: Your Clappia Workplace ID
    
- requestingUserEmailAddress: Email of user on whose behalf the search query will be made
    

**For Pagination:**

- forward: true for next pages, false for previous
    
- pageSize: Number of submissions per page (max 100)
    

**Filters:**

- Use queries for complex AND/OR logic
    
- filterKeyType: 'STANDARD' for built-in fields, 'CUSTOM' for app fields
    
- Operators: EQ, GTE, LTE, CONTAINS, etc.
    
- Check examples below

item.description:
API to filter and download submissions as Excel.

Advanced submission querying with:

- **Filtering:** By status, dates, custom fields
    
- **Sorting:** Order by any field or timestamp
    
- **Complex Logic:** AND/OR conditions, nested queries
    

**Operators:** EQ, GTE, LTE, CONTAINS, IN, NOT_IN

**Required Fields:**

- appId: Unique identifier of your Clappia App
    
- workplaceId: Your Clappia Workplace ID
    
- requestingUserEmailAddress: Email of user on whose behalf the search query will be made
    

**Filters:**

- filters: Refer to the Get Submissions Request for the exact structure of filters

request.description:
Update basic properties of an existing field such as label, description, validation rules, and display settings.

**Updatable Properties:**
- label: Display name of the field
- description: Help text for users
- required: Make field mandatory/optional
- validation: Change validation rules
- Block width percentages for responsive design

**Use Cases:**
- Refining field labels and descriptions
- Changing validation requirements
- Adjusting field layout and responsiveness

{
    "appId": "YOUR_APP_ID",
    "fieldName": "weather_data",
    "fieldType": "getDataFromRestApis",
    "label": "Current Weather",
    "description": "Fetch current weather conditions",
    "required": false,
    "blockWidthPercentageDesktop": 100,
    "blockWidthPercentageMobile": 100,
    "isEditable": false,
    "retainValues": true,
    "serverUrl": "https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather",
    "methodType": "GET",
    "headers": "{\"X-API-Key\": \"YOUR_OPENWEATHER_KEY\", \"Accept\": \"application/json\"}",
    "queryString": "q={location}&appid=YOUR_API_KEY&units=imperial",
    "responseMapping": [
        {
            "name": "temperature",
            "dataType": "numberInput",
            "jsonPathQuery": "$.main.temp",
            "xPathQuery": "//main/temp"
        },
        {
            "name": "humidity",
            "dataType": "numberInput",
            "jsonPathQuery": "$.main.humidity",
            "xPathQuery": "//main/humidity"
        },
        {
            "name": "description",
            "dataType": "singleLineText",
            "jsonPathQuery": "$.weather[0].description",
            "xPathQuery": "//weather/description"
        }
    ]
}

- Adding new data collection categories

request.description:
Move a section from its current position to a new position in the app. All sections will automatically adjust their positions.

**Reordering Logic:**
- Section is removed from current position
- Section is inserted at new position
- All other sections shift up or down as needed
- All fields within the section move with it

**Use Cases:**
- Moving important sections to the top
- Reorganizing form flow for better UX
- Grouping related sections together
- Optimizing form completion rates

Insert a page break after a specific section to create multi-page forms. Improves user experience for long forms by breaking them into manageable pages.

**Page Break Configuration:**
- afterSectionIndex: Section after which to insert page break
- pageTitle: Title for the new page
- pageDescription: Optional description/instructions
- showProgressBar: Display form completion progress
- allowBackNavigation: Enable previous page navigation

**Use Cases:**
- Breaking long forms into digestible pages
- Creating step-by-step workflows
- Separating different data collection phases
- Improving mobile form experience

item.name: Put Object in the Presigned URL (S3 bucket)

request.description:
## Generate File Download URL

This endpoint generates a download URL for any file uploaded in Clappia. Given a file identifier and upload category, it returns a pre-signed URL that can be used to download the file.

---

## `uploadCategory`

The `uploadCategory` parameter specifies the type/category of the uploaded file. To download a file from a **Clappia App Submission**, pass `uploadCategory` as `"submissionData"`.

All allowed values are listed below:

- submissionData
    
- appIcon
    
- printTemplateHtml
    
- htmlBlockFile
    
- printTemplateFile
    
- attachedFile
    
- imageAttachedFile
    
- videoAttachedFile
    
- pdfAttachedFile
    

---

## `fileId`

The `fileId` is the unique identifier for the file you want to download. This value is received from the **`getSubmissions`** or **`getSubmission`** endpoint as part of the submission data.

---

## Sample cURL Request

``` bash
curl --location --request POST '{{baseUrl}}/file/generateFileDownloadUrl' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'x-api-key: <your-api-key>' \
-d '{
  "appId": "APP123",
  "fileId": "Use File Id received from GetSubmissions or GetSubmission API",
  "uploadCategory": "submissionData"
}'

 ```
